message:
feat(spec): Apply 12 protocol design review revisions to FCP V2

This commit incorporates comprehensive protocol/security/systems design
review feedback targeting robustness, performance, and real-world operability
while maintaining alignment with the three foundational axioms.

## Core Object Model & Cryptographic Foundations

### 1. Threshold Owner Signing (§2.2.1)
- Add OwnerKeyPolicy, OwnerKeyScheme, and OwnerKeyShare structs
- Owner key is now "key role 0" with FROST threshold signing RECOMMENDED
- Single device compromise no longer equals owner compromise
- Provides catastrophic compromise resistance and loss tolerance

### 2. ObjectId/Header Split (§3.6)
- Remove object_id and retention from ObjectHeader
- Add StorageMeta for node-local storage policy (retention is NOT content-addressed)
- Add StoredObject with canonical_bytes() and derive_id() methods
- Fix content-addressing ambiguity: ObjectId derives from (header, body)
- Update GC code to use get_storage_meta() instead of header.retention

### 3. Monotonic Seq Numbers for Freshness (§7.5, §14.3, §23.4)
- Add rev_seq to CapabilityToken for O(1) freshness comparison
- Add seq to RevocationEvent and AuditEvent
- Add head_seq to RevocationHead and AuditHead
- Add ZoneFrontier checkpoint for fast sync without chain traversal
- Eliminates expensive O(n) chain traversal for freshness checks

## Protocol Performance Optimizations

### 4. Session MACs Instead of Per-Frame Signatures (§4.2)
- Add MeshSessionHello, MeshSessionAck for X25519 ECDH handshake
- Add AuthenticatedFcpsFrame with Poly1305 MAC + monotonic seq
- Retain SignedFcpsFrame for bootstrap/degraded mode
- Amortizes Ed25519 cost over many frames (critical for mobile)

### 5. Nonce Derivation from frame_nonce_base + ESI (§4.1, §4.3)
- Remove per-symbol nonce field from SymbolEnvelope
- Add 8-byte frame_nonce_base to FCPS header (bytes 82-89)
- Derive nonce as frame_nonce_base || esi_le (deterministic)
- Saves 12 bytes/symbol bandwidth and eliminates per-symbol RNG overhead
- Update header size from 82 to 90 bytes

## Security Enforcement Improvements

### 6. Egress Proxy + CIDR Deny Defaults (§7.1, §11.2)
- Add network.egress capability (default for strict/moderate sandboxes)
- Add ip_allow, cidr_deny, deny_localhost, deny_private_ranges,
  deny_tailnet_ranges to NetworkConstraints
- Connectors in strict/moderate profiles MUST NOT have raw socket syscalls
- Network access goes through MeshNode egress proxy enforcing constraints
- Prevents SSRF into localhost/tailnet/RFC1918 unless explicitly allowed

### 7. Taint Reduction with Proofs (§6.1, §6.2)
- Add taint_reductions: Vec<TaintReduction> to Provenance
- Add TaintReduction struct with attestation ObjectId proof
- Add effective_taint() method that applies reductions
- Update can_invoke() to use effective_taint()
- Prevents "approve everything" fatigue from taint-only-accumulates

### 8. Unified ApprovalToken (§5.2, §6.3)
- Replace ElevationToken and DeclassificationToken with ApprovalToken
- Add ApprovalScope enum (Elevation, Declassification)
- Update InvokeRequest to use approval_tokens: Vec<ApprovalToken>
- Update MeshNode verification to find appropriate approval by scope
- Simplifies UI, audit, verification code paths, and policy

## Operational Features

### 9. Control Plane Retention by Class (§9.3)
- Add retention class table (Required vs Ephemeral)
- Add ControlPlaneRetention enum
- Required: invoke, response, receipts, approvals, audit
- Ephemeral: health, handshake, decode_status, symbol_ack
- Reduces storage churn and gossip overhead

### 10. Connector State Objects + Single-Writer (§10.4)
- Add ConnectorStateRoot and ConnectorStateObject structs
- Add singleton_writer manifest option
- Local $CONNECTOR_STATE is cache only; mesh objects are authoritative
- Enables safe failover, resumable polling, deterministic migration

### 11. Supply Chain Attestations (§13.1, §13.2)
- Add [supply_chain] and [policy] manifest sections
- Add SupplyChainAttestation, AttestationType, SupplyChainPolicy
- Support in-toto statements with SLSA provenance predicates
- Verification chain includes attestation and SLSA checks
- Makes provenance machine-checkable, not just "signature valid"

### 12. ObjectPlacementPolicy + Repair Controller (§3.6, §21.3)
- Add placement: Option<ObjectPlacementPolicy> to ObjectHeader
- Add ObjectPlacementPolicy with min_nodes, max_node_fraction,
  preferred_devices, excluded_devices, target_coverage
- Add RepairController and CoverageEvaluation for background repair
- Turns "offline resilience" into a measurable, maintainable SLO

## Meta-Architecture Result

These changes establish a clean three-layer enforcement stack:
1. Transport/channel authentication (session MACs, anti-replay)
2. Zone crypto (AEAD symbols, deterministic key selection, rotation-safe)
3. Authority & effects control (cap tokens, approvals, receipts, revocations)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
